title: Buildah does not create config for containers
body: When buildah loads a container, it does not create a config.json file in $ROOT/[driver]-containers/[container-id]/userdata.  cri-o and kpod use this file to load containers into the container store

comments.body: @nalind @mrunalp The question here is how do we get kpod/crio and buildah to potentially share containers.  Do we create a config.json with some defaults for buildah?

We don't maintain a bundle for `runc`, since we'd also need to regenerate it whenever `buildah config` was run.  Instead, we just generate one in a temporary directory every time we need to run `runc`.
So should kpod realize this and treat this as a container without a runc config?

Alternatively, is there a way to get the path to the temporary buildah directory from the buildah.json?  Also, where are the temporary directories?  I have two buildah containers running on my machine but I can't seem to find the temporary directories associated with them
The container's `BundlePath()` method should return the directory where the file is located, at least while it's running.
Will that work for kpod/crio containers as well?
To clarify, `BundlePath()` is a method of `src/github.com/kubernetes-incubator/cri-o/oci.Container`.
If the config.json needs to be accessed before the oci Container is created, there a way to determine the bundle path?
This caused issues with kpod ps and kpod inspect.  Kpod can't inspect running buildah containers because they don't contain that file with some of the necessary information.
Closing this as we've  a Trello card to make Buildah/Podman play nicely together which includes listing.  https://trello.com/c/0eUqT0uG
